Submodules¶

custom_tools.custom_auprc module¶

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing module¶

class custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.MudataProcessor(mdata, processed_data_dir, sample_name, tss_path, tf_list_file=None)[source]¶

Bases: object

atac_qc_filter(min_cells_per_peak=20, min_peaks_per_cell=500, max_peaks_per_cell=2500, min_total_counts_per_cell=1000, max_total_counts_per_cell=5000, scale_factor=10000.0, min_atac_disp=0.5, min_atac_hvg_mean=0.05, max_atac_hvg_mean=1.5, promoter_upstream=1000, promoter_downstream=100, distal_max=200000, filter_hvgs: bool = True, n_neighbors: int = 10, n_pcs: int = 30, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶
construct_peak_annotation(save_dir: Path, promoter_upstream: int = 1000, promoter_downstream: int = 100, distal_max: int = 200000)[source]¶

Construct a peak annotation table in 10x-style format, assigning each peak to a gene and distance based on TSS proximity.

flag_tfs_to_keep()[source]¶
nucleosome_signal(frag_path: Path, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶
rna_pca_and_neighbors(rna, n_pcs=20, n_neighbors=10, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶

Perform principal component analysis (PCA) and k-nearest neighbors (kNN) on the RNA data.

Parameters:

  • rna (ad.AnnData) – The RNA data.

  • n_pcs (int, optional) – The number of principal components to keep. Defaults to 20.

  • n_neighbors (int, optional) – The number of k-nearest neighbors to keep. Defaults to 10.

  • fig_dir (Path|None, optional) – The directory to save the figures in. Defaults to None.

Return type:

Nothing. The function modifies the RNA data in-place.

rna_qc_filter(min_cells_per_gene: int = 20, min_genes_per_cell: int = 500, max_genes_per_cell: int = 2500, min_total_counts_per_cell: int = 1000, max_total_counts_per_cell: int = 5000, max_pct_counts_mt: int = 20, norm_target_sum: float = 10000.0, min_rna_disp: float = 0.5, min_rna_hvg_mean: float = 0.02, max_rna_hvg_mean: float = 4, filter_hvgs: bool = True, tf_list_file: Path | None = None, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶

Filter RNA data based on quality control criteria.

Parameters:

  • min_cells_per_gene (int, optional) – Minimum number of cells a gene must be present in to be kept. Defaults to 20.

  • min_genes_per_cell (int, optional) – Minimum number of genes a cell must have to be kept. Defaults to 500.

  • max_genes_per_cell (int, optional) – Maximum number of genes a cell can have to be kept. Defaults to 2500.

  • min_total_counts_per_cell (int, optional) – Minimum total number of counts a cell must have to be kept. Defaults to 1000.

  • max_total_counts_per_cell (int, optional) – Maximum total number of counts a cell can have to be kept. Defaults to 5000.

  • max_pct_counts_mt (int, optional) – Maximum percentage of counts a cell can have in mitochondrial genes to be kept. Defaults to 20.

Return type:

Nothing. The function modifies the RNA data in-place.

save_atac()[source]¶
save_mdata()[source]¶
save_rna()[source]¶
save_stability_subsamplings(raw_mdata: MuData, subsampling_dir: Path, pct_subsample: float = 0.7, num_subsamples: int = 10)[source]¶
show_pre_filtering_qc_atac()[source]¶
show_pre_filtering_qc_rna()[source]¶
tss_enrichment(frag_path: Path, n_tss: int = 500, extend_upstream: int = 1000, extend_downstream: int = 1000, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶
tss_enrichment_plot(data: AnnData, color: str | None = None, title: str = 'TSS Enrichment', ax: Axes | None = None, save: str | None = None)[source]¶

Plot relative enrichment scores around a TSS.

Parameters:

  • data – AnnData object with cell x TSS_position matrix as generated by muon.atac.tl.tss_enrichment.

  • color – Column name of .obs slot of the AnnData object which to group TSS signals by.

  • title – Plot title.

  • ax – A matplotlib axes object.

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.construct_mdata_from_gene_by_cell_matrices(rna_count_file: Path, atac_count_file: Path) → MuData[source]¶

Construct a MuData object from gene-by-cell matrices for RNA and ATAC data.

Parameters:

  • rna_count_file (Path) – Path to the RNA count matrix file (genes as rows, cells as columns).

  • atac_count_file (Path) – Path to the ATAC count matrix file (peaks as rows, cells as columns).

Returns:

A MuData object containing the RNA and ATAC data.

Return type:

mu.MuData

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.create_fragment_index_file(frag_path: Path) → str[source]¶

Create a tabix index file for a fragments.tsv.gz file if it doesn’t already exist.

Parameters:

frag_path (Path) – Path to the fragments.tsv.gz file.

Returns:

Path to the created or existing index file (fragments.tsv.gz.tbi).

Return type:

str

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.create_metacells(mdata: AnnData, sample_processed_data_dir: Path, hops: int = 2)[source]¶

Create metacell-level profiles from RNA and ATAC data matrices.

Parameters:

  • mdata (ad.AnnData) – The MuData object containing RNA and ATAC data matrices.

  • sample_processed_data_dir (Path) – The directory where the pseudobulk DataFrames will be saved.

  • hops (int, default=2) – The number of hops between neighbors to consider when diffusing information.

Return type:

None

Notes

This function creates metacell-level profiles by applying a diffusion operator to the RNA and ATAC data matrices. The diffusion operator is constructed by first extracting the neighbor graph from the MuData object and converting it to a row-normalized sparse matrix. The operator is then applied to the RNA and ATAC data matrices to obtain the metacell-level profiles. The resulting profiles are saved as parquet files in the specified directory.

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.filter_to_human(mdata)[source]¶

Filter a barnyard MuData object to hg38 ATAC peaks only, then strip the ‘hg38.’ prefix from peak IDs.

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.get_threshold(sample_filtering_settings, setting_name, verbose=True)[source]¶
custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.integrate_rna_atac(mdata: AnnData, sample_processed_data_dir: Path, sample_name: str, fig_dir: Path | None = None)[source]¶
custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.load_raw_data(sample_name: str, sample_data_dir: Path, rna_count_file: Path | None = None, atac_count_file: Path | None = None, raw_h5_file: Path | None = None, verbose: bool = True)[source]¶
custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.normalize_peak_format(peak_id: str) → str[source]¶

Normalize peak format from chrN-start-end or chrN:start:end to chrN:start-end. Handles both formats as input and always outputs chrN:start-end.

custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.parse_args()[source]¶
custom_tools.muon_preprocessing.save_processed_data(mdata: AnnData, sample_processed_data_dir: Path)[source]¶

custom_tools.onehot_dna module¶

custom_tools.onehot_dna.create_centered_peak_onehot_array(peak_ids: list[str], genome_fasta: str | ~pathlib.Path, chrom_sizes: dict[str, int], peak_id_to_idx: dict[str, int], flank_size: int, dtype=<class 'numpy.uint8'>, pad_out_of_bounds: bool = True, show_progress: bool = True, num_workers: int = 1, chunk_size: int = 1000)[source]¶

Create a stacked one-hot encoded DNA array using an existing peak_id_to_idx map.

Parameters:

  • peak_ids (list[str]) – Peak IDs to encode. These should all exist in peak_id_to_idx.

  • genome_fasta (str | Path) – Path to genome FASTA.

  • chrom_sizes (dict[str, int]) – Dictionary mapping chromosome names to chromosome sizes.

  • peak_id_to_idx (dict[str, int]) – Existing mapping from peak_id -> row index.

  • flank_size (int) – Number of bases on each side of the peak center. Output length is 2 * flank_size.

  • dtype (numpy dtype) – Output dtype. np.uint8 is recommended for one-hot DNA.

  • pad_out_of_bounds (bool) – Whether to pad with N if the requested window goes out of bounds.

  • show_progress (bool) – Whether to show tqdm progress bar.

  • num_workers (int) – Number of worker processes to use. Use 1 to run serially.

  • chunk_size (int) – Number of peaks per worker task when num_workers > 1.

Returns:

Array of shape [len(peak_id_to_idx), 2 * flank_size, 4].

Return type:

np.ndarray

custom_tools.onehot_dna.load_chrom_sizes(chromsizes_file: str | Path) → dict[str, int][source]¶

Load chromosome sizes from a chrom.sizes file.

Parameters:

chromsizes_file (str | Path) – Path to the chrom.sizes file.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping chromosome names to sizes.

Return type:

dict

custom_tools.onehot_dna.load_peak_sequence(genome_fasta: str | Path, selected_peak: str) → str[source]¶

Load the DNA sequence for a given peak.

Parameters:

  • genome_fasta (str | Path) – Path to the genome fasta file.

  • selected_peak (str) – Peak string in the format “chrom:start-end”.

Returns:

The DNA sequence for the peak.

Return type:

str

custom_tools.onehot_dna.onehot_dna_sequence(seq: str) → ndarray[source]¶

Fast one-hot encoding for DNA sequence.

Parameters:

seq (str) – DNA sequence consisting of characters A, C, G, T (case-insensitive).

Returns:

Shape: (L, 4), dtype float32

Return type:

np.ndarray

custom_tools.onehot_dna.parse_peak(peak: str) → tuple[str, int, int][source]¶

Parse peak string like chr1:100-200.

Parameters:

peak (str) – Peak string in the format “chrom:start-end”.

Returns:

(chromosome, start, end)

Return type:

tuple[str, int, int]

custom_tools.peak_utils module¶

custom_tools.peak_utils.find_genes_near_peaks(peak_bed: BedTool, tss_bed: BedTool, tss_distance_cutoff: int | float = 1000000.0) → DataFrame[source]¶

Identify genes whose transcription start sites (TSS) are near scATAC-seq peaks.

This function:

  1. Uses BedTools to find peaks that are within tss_distance_cutoff bp of each gene’s TSS.

  2. Converts the BedTool result to a pandas DataFrame.

  3. Computes the absolute distance between the peak end and gene start (as a proxy for TSS distance).

Parameters:

  • peak_bed (pybedtools.BedTool) – BedTool object with scATAC-seq peaks.

  • tss_bed (pybedtools.BedTool) – BedTool object with gene TSS locations.

  • tss_distance_cutoff (int|float) – The maximum distance (in bp) from a TSS to consider a peak as potentially regulatory.

Returns:

A DataFrame containing columns “peak_id”, “target_id”, and the scaled TSS distance “TSS_dist” for peak–gene pairs.

Return type:

peak_tss_subset_df (pandas.DataFrame)

custom_tools.peak_utils.format_individual_peak(peak_id: str) → str[source]¶

Normalize peak format from chrN-start-end or chrN:start:end to chrN:start-end. Handles both formats as input and always outputs chrN:start-end.

custom_tools.peak_utils.format_peak_dataframe(peak_ids: Series | Index) → DataFrame[source]¶

Splits peaks from chrN:start-end or chrN-start-end format into a DataFrame.

Creates a dataframe with the following columns: 1) “peak_id”: peakN+1 where N is the index position of the peak 2) “chromosome”: chrN 3) “start” 4) “end” 5) “strand”: List of “.” values, we dont have strand information for our peaks.

Parameters:

peak_ids (pd.Series) – Series containing the peak locations in “chrN:start-end” or “chrN-start-end” format.

Returns:

DataFrame of peak locations in the correct format for Homer and the sliding window method

Return type:

peak_df (pd.DataFrame)

custom_tools.peak_utils.get_peak_length(peak_id_col: Series) → Series[source]¶

Finds the base pair lengths for a Series of genomic ranges in chr:start-end format.

Parameters:

peak_id_col (pd.Series) – Series of genomic locations in chr:start-end format.

Returns:

base pair lengths of the genomic ranges proviced.

Return type:

pd.Series

custom_tools.peak_utils.set_tg_as_closest_gene_tss_to_peak(tf_peak_edge_df: DataFrame, peaks_gene_distance_file: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

set the target gene (TG) for each TF-peak edge as the closest gene to the peak based on TSS distance.

Parameters:

  • tf_peak_edge_df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame containing TF-peak edges with a “peak_id” column.

  • peaks_gene_distance_file (str) – Path to a parquet file containing peak-to-gene distance information with columns “peak_id”, “target_id”, and “TSS_dist_score”.

Returns:

DataFrame with the target gene (TG) for each TF-peak edge set as the closest gene to the peak based on TSS distance.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame
